metadata.name: amm
metadata.description: Dr Fraudsworth AMM Program - Constant-product swap pools

instructions.name: burn_admin
docs: Burns the admin key, permanently preventing new pool creation.
Only the current admin can call this. Irreversible.
# Accounts
* `admin` - Current admin signer
* `admin_config` - AdminConfig PDA (admin set to Pubkey::default())

accounts.name: admin
docs: The current admin. Must match admin_config.admin.

accounts.name: admin_config
docs: The AdminConfig PDA. After this instruction, admin will be Pubkey::default().

instructions.name: initialize_admin
docs: Initialize the global AdminConfig PDA.
Can only be called by the program's upgrade authority (deployer).
The `admin` parameter sets who can create pools -- this can be a
different key from the upgrade authority (e.g., a multisig).

accounts.name: authority
docs: The program's upgrade authority. Must sign to prove deployer identity.

accounts.name: admin_config
docs: The AdminConfig PDA. Initialized once; stores the admin pubkey and bump.
Seeds: [b"admin"]

accounts.name: program
docs: The AMM program itself -- used to look up its programdata address.

accounts.name: program_data
docs: The program's ProgramData account (created by the BPF loader on deploy).
Constraint: its upgrade_authority_address must equal the signing authority.
This is how we verify the caller is the deployer.

instructions.name: initialize_pool
docs: Initialize a new AMM pool with PDA-owned vaults and seed liquidity.
Creates pool state PDA, vault token accounts (owned by pool PDA),
and transfers initial liquidity atomically. Pool type is inferred
from token programs, not caller-declared.
# Arguments
* `lp_fee_bps` - LP fee in basis points
* `amount_a` - Initial seed amount for token A
* `amount_b` - Initial seed amount for token B

accounts.name: payer
docs: The payer for account rent. Typically the admin, but can be a separate funder.

accounts.name: admin_config
docs: The global AdminConfig PDA. Verified via has_one = admin constraint
to ensure only the authorized admin can create pools.

accounts.name: admin
docs: The admin signer. Must match admin_config.admin.
Also acts as the authority for the initial liquidity transfers.

accounts.name: pool
docs: The pool state PDA. Derived from canonical mint pair.
Seeds: [b"pool", mint_a.key().as_ref(), mint_b.key().as_ref()]

accounts.name: vault_a
docs: Vault A: PDA-owned token account for reserve A.
The pool PDA is the authority, ensuring only the program can move funds.
Seeds: [b"vault", pool.key().as_ref(), b"a"]

accounts.name: vault_b
docs: Vault B: PDA-owned token account for reserve B.
Seeds: [b"vault", pool.key().as_ref(), b"b"]

accounts.name: mint_a
docs: Mint A (the canonically smaller pubkey).
Constraint: on-chain owner must match the provided token_program_a.
This prevents passing a T22 mint with the SPL Token program (or vice versa).

accounts.name: mint_b
docs: Mint B (the canonically larger pubkey).
Same owner validation as mint_a.

accounts.name: source_a
docs: Admin's source token account for mint A.
Must have sufficient balance for the initial seed amount.

accounts.name: source_b
docs: Admin's source token account for mint B.

accounts.name: token_program_a
docs: Token program for mint A (SPL Token or Token-2022).

accounts.name: token_program_b
docs: Token program for mint B (SPL Token or Token-2022).

instructions.name: swap_sol_pool
docs: Execute a swap in a SOL pool (CRIME/SOL or FRAUD/SOL).
Routes between Token-2022 (CRIME/FRAUD) and SPL Token (WSOL) based on
swap direction. LP fee is deducted before output calculation.
# Arguments
* `amount_in` - Input token amount (pre-fee)
* `direction` - SwapDirection::AtoB or SwapDirection::BtoA
* `minimum_amount_out` - Slippage protection floor

accounts.name: swap_authority
docs: swap_authority PDA: must be signed by Tax Program via invoke_signed.
The Signer type validates this account actually signed the transaction.
The seeds + seeds::program constraint validates the PDA is derived
from TAX_PROGRAM_ID with seeds ["swap_authority"].
This ensures only the Tax Program can initiate swaps -- direct user
calls without valid swap_authority will fail deserialization.

accounts.name: pool
docs: Pool state PDA. Mutable for reserve updates and reentrancy guard.
Seeds validate this is the correct pool for the given mint pair.

accounts.name: vault_a
docs: Vault A: PDA-owned token account holding reserve A.
Validated against pool state to prevent vault substitution attacks.

accounts.name: vault_b
docs: Vault B: PDA-owned token account holding reserve B.

accounts.name: mint_a
docs: Mint A: used for decimals in transfer_checked and token program routing.

accounts.name: mint_b
docs: Mint B: used for decimals in transfer_checked and token program routing.

accounts.name: user_token_a
docs: User's token account for token A. Mutable for input or output transfers.
No ownership constraint -- the token program validates authority during
transfer_checked CPI (see 11-RESEARCH.md Open Question 3).

accounts.name: user_token_b
docs: User's token account for token B.

accounts.name: user
docs: The user executing the swap. Signs as authority for user-to-vault transfers.

accounts.name: token_program_a
docs: Token program for mint A (SPL Token or Token-2022).
Validated against pool state to prevent program substitution.

accounts.name: token_program_b
docs: Token program for mint B (SPL Token or Token-2022).

instructions.name: transfer_admin
docs: Transfer the admin key to a new pubkey (e.g., Squads multisig vault).
Only the current admin can call this. new_admin must not be Pubkey::default().

accounts.name: admin
docs: The current admin. Must match admin_config.admin.

accounts.name: admin_config
docs: The AdminConfig PDA. After this instruction, admin will be new_admin.

errors.name: KInvariantViolation
errors.msg: K-invariant violation: k decreased after swap

errors.name: MintsNotCanonicallyOrdered
errors.msg: Mints must be in canonical order (mint_a < mint_b)

errors.name: Unauthorized
errors.msg: Unauthorized: signer is not the admin

errors.name: InvalidTokenProgram
errors.msg: Token program does not match mint owner

errors.name: ZeroSeedAmount
errors.msg: Initial seed amount must be greater than zero

errors.name: DuplicateMints
errors.msg: Mint A and Mint B must be different

errors.name: ZeroAmount
errors.msg: Transfer amount must be greater than zero

errors.name: VaultMismatch
errors.msg: Vault does not match pool state

errors.name: InvalidMint
errors.msg: Mint does not match pool state

errors.name: ZeroEffectiveInput
errors.msg: Input amount too small: fee deduction produces zero effective input

errors.name: InvalidSwapAuthority
errors.msg: Swaps must go through Tax Program - direct calls not allowed

errors.name: LpFeeExceedsMax
errors.msg: LP fee exceeds maximum allowed (500 bps)

errors.name: InvalidAuthority
errors.msg: Invalid authority: cannot transfer to Pubkey::default()

types.name: AdminBurned
docs: Emitted when the admin key is permanently burned.
After this event, no new pools can be created through the AMM.
This is irreversible.

fields.name: burned_by
docs: The admin who burned their own key.

fields.name: slot
docs: Slot when the burn occurred.

types.name: AdminConfig
docs: Global admin configuration for the AMM program.
This PDA is initialized once by the program's upgrade authority,
storing the admin pubkey that gates pool creation. The admin can
be a multisig address for operational security.
Seeds: [b"admin"]

fields.name: admin
docs: The admin pubkey authorized to create pools.
Can be a multisig address -- not required to be the upgrade authority.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump seed for re-derivation in downstream instructions.

types.name: PoolInitializedEvent
docs: Emitted when a new pool is initialized with its first liquidity.
`pool_type` is serialized as u8 for client compatibility:
- 0 = MixedPool (one SPL Token + one Token-2022)
- 1 = PureT22Pool (both Token-2022)

fields.name: pool
docs: The pool PDA address.

fields.name: pool_type
docs: Pool type as u8 (0 = MixedPool, 1 = PureT22Pool).

fields.name: mint_a
docs: Canonical mint A (the "smaller" pubkey).

fields.name: mint_b
docs: Canonical mint B (the "larger" pubkey).

fields.name: vault_a
docs: Vault A PDA address.

fields.name: vault_b
docs: Vault B PDA address.

fields.name: reserve_a
docs: Initial reserve of token A after seeding.

fields.name: reserve_b
docs: Initial reserve of token B after seeding.

fields.name: lp_fee_bps
docs: LP fee in basis points.

types.name: PoolState
docs: On-chain state for a single AMM pool.
Each pool is a PDA derived from its canonical mint pair:
Seeds: [b"pool", mint_a.as_ref(), mint_b.as_ref()]
Canonical ordering: mint_a < mint_b (byte-wise pubkey comparison).
This ensures exactly one pool PDA per unordered mint pair.
Space: 8 (discriminator) + 1 (pool_type) + 32*2 (mints) + 32*2 (vaults)
+ 8*2 (reserves) + 2 (fee) + 1 (initialized) + 1 (locked)
+ 1 (bump) + 1 (vault_a_bump) + 1 (vault_b_bump)
+ 32*2 (token_programs)
= 8 + 216 = 224 bytes total (216 INIT_SPACE).
DEVIATION from AMM_Implementation.md Section 4.1 (157 bytes):
We store vault bumps (2 bytes) and token program keys (64 bytes)
on-chain, adding 66 bytes. This avoids re-deriving vault PDAs and
re-validating token programs during every swap, reducing compute cost.

fields.name: pool_type
docs: Behavioral pool type (MixedPool or PureT22Pool).

fields.name: mint_a
docs: First mint in the canonical pair (mint_a < mint_b).

fields.name: mint_b
docs: Second mint in the canonical pair.

fields.name: vault_a
docs: PDA-owned token account holding reserve A.

fields.name: vault_b
docs: PDA-owned token account holding reserve B.

fields.name: reserve_a
docs: Current reserve of token A (updated on every swap/deposit).

fields.name: reserve_b
docs: Current reserve of token B (updated on every swap/deposit).

fields.name: lp_fee_bps
docs: LP fee in basis points (e.g., 100 = 1.0%).

fields.name: initialized
docs: Whether the pool has been fully initialized with liquidity.

fields.name: locked
docs: Reentrancy guard. Set to true during swap execution, cleared after.
SPEC DEVIATION from AMM_Implementation.md: This field is not in the
original spec. Added for defense-in-depth reentrancy protection per
11-CONTEXT.md. Solana's runtime borrow rules already prevent same-pool
re-entry via CPI, and CEI ordering handles reserve consistency, but
this provides an explicit belt-and-suspenders guard.

fields.name: bump
docs: Pool PDA bump seed.

fields.name: vault_a_bump
docs: Vault A PDA bump seed (avoids re-derivation in swaps).

fields.name: vault_b_bump
docs: Vault B PDA bump seed (avoids re-derivation in swaps).

fields.name: token_program_a
docs: Token program for mint A (SPL Token or Token-2022).

fields.name: token_program_b
docs: Token program for mint B (SPL Token or Token-2022).

types.name: PoolType
docs: Behavioral pool type, inferred from the token programs of each mint.
DEVIATION from AMM_Implementation.md Section 4.1:
The spec originally defined four protocol-specific variants. Per
09-CONTEXT.md, the AMM is mint-agnostic -- it accepts any mint pair
and categorizes by token program combination, not by protocol identity.
- `MixedPool`: One side uses SPL Token, the other uses Token-2022.
Example: CRIME/SOL (T22 + SPL), FRAUD/SOL (T22 + SPL).
- `PureT22Pool`: Both sides use Token-2022.
Reserved for future use; no active pools use this variant.

types.name: SwapDirection
docs: Direction of a swap through a pool.
Anchor serializes this as a single u8 variant index:
- 0 = AtoB (Token A in, Token B out)
- 1 = BtoA (Token B in, Token A out)
The caller explicitly declares direction. The AMM does not infer it from
account ordering (locked decision, see 11-CONTEXT.md).

types.name: SwapEvent
docs: Emitted when a swap executes successfully in a SOL pool.
Contains all information needed for indexers and frontends to track
swap activity without additional RPC lookups. Direction is encoded
as u8 for client compatibility (0 = AtoB, 1 = BtoA).
`lp_fee_bps` is intentionally omitted -- it is immutable on pool state,
so clients can query it once and cache. See 11-CONTEXT.md.

fields.name: pool
docs: The pool PDA address.

fields.name: user
docs: The user who initiated the swap.

fields.name: input_mint
docs: Mint of the input token.

fields.name: output_mint
docs: Mint of the output token.

fields.name: amount_in
docs: Amount of input token (pre-fee).

fields.name: amount_out
docs: Amount of output token sent to user.

fields.name: lp_fee
docs: LP fee deducted (in input token units).

fields.name: reserve_a
docs: Post-swap reserve of token A.

fields.name: reserve_b
docs: Post-swap reserve of token B.

fields.name: direction
docs: Swap direction (0 = AtoB, 1 = BtoA).

fields.name: timestamp
docs: Unix timestamp from Clock sysvar.

fields.name: slot
docs: Slot from Clock sysvar.
